title: The Station's Basement, Archive Depth

features.name: perception-ceased-here
features.desc-on-touch: The archive's sensors ended at the platform above. Below this line, there is no official record, only what the dark remembers. But memory, you find, is just another sense.

features.name: blind-apparatus
features.desc-on-touch: Machinery that works in darkness, needing no eyes. The archive learned to listen instead of looking, and found that the basement was never empty—it was always full of voices.

features.name: touch-filing
features.desc-on-touch: Files organized by texture, temperature, and sound. The hand knows what the eye cannot see. Documents stored in the dark become readable only through touch.

features.name: depth-boundary
features.desc-on-touch: The line where the archive's official records end and the basement's unofficial archive begins. Above: catalogued. Below: felt, remembered, sensed.

features.name: archive-continues-here
features.desc-on-touch: The basement is not empty because perception ceased. The archive continues here by other means: by touch, by vibration, by the sounds that only darkness can make.

inscription: the archive's eyes gave up here, but the darkness learned to see through other senses; perception ended but the archive did not stop, it only changed how it listens

file.name: Station_Basement_Unlogged_Hours.mp3
file.note: Seventeen years of ambient recording from the basement after the archive's sensors failed. Pure sound: dripping water, settling concrete, machinery learning to speak in the dark.

exits.label: back up to the station where sight still exists
deeper into the subbasement where archive forgets what it is
toward the margin where touch becomes archive
into the vault where blind apparatus still works
